active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 9 diagnostic-first bundle for replaying and classifying the 20 phase-8 LoCoMo failures from real v3 public benchmark reports, while preserving v3 default, v1 fallback, and kernel opt-in.

objective: Create complete failure replay artifacts and taxonomy for phase-8 LoCoMo failures before Phase 10 recall changes.
failing_tests_required_before_implementa: Add a focused RED test proving a real phase-8 LoCoMo failed report row can be transformed only after the replay schema/generator exposes all required fields.
Add a focused RED test proving required path-level classes remain distinct from aggregate report failure classes.
Do not modify answer or retrieval behavior unless the RED test shows current real-path diagnostics cannot classify a real case.
milestone_eval_policy: Phase 9 is diagnostic-first. 30-case LLM judge gates are not required unless real public benchmark behavior changes. If behavior changes are made, run LongMemEval and LoCoMo 30-case full-chain gates in parallel with heartbeat artifacts.
usable_ack_checklist: all 20 phase-8 LoCoMo failed cases are classified

each failed case has replay row/artifact with case id, question, expected source ids, indexed source status, retrieved ids, selected ids, rendered ids, answer output, cited/source support, judge result, movement status, report-level failure class, path-level failure class, and diagnostic notes
diagnostic_gap cases are explicit and not hidden
source metrics are separated from judged answer metrics
tests and ruff pass or failures are explicitly blocking
v1 fallback, v3 default, and kernel opt-in remain unchanged
no demo-only or aggregate-only promotion claim is made
review_focus: context bundle was read and cited by plan, execute, and review outputs
replay artifacts consume real phase-8 v3 public benchmark report rows
LoCoMo failure classes are case-level and not hidden by aggregate pass rate
no answer/retrieval behavior changes are smuggled into a diagnostic phase
source grounding and citation support are separated from judged answer pass/fail
kernel default remains off and v1 fallback remains explicit
no case-id hacks or expected-answer leaks
decision_rule: Only ack_level=usable may advance. Otherwise write adjustment.md and repeat or narrow Phase 9.
